name: Niger
avoid: March onward turns brutally hot, and June to September can wash out rougher roads around the Aïr and W National Park.
Safety advisory: multiple governments currently advise against travel to Niger; recheck live security guidance, use hotel-arranged airport transfers, and avoid independent road movement after dark.
Bush taxis still handle many paved intercity legs, but desert travel beyond Agadez needs a properly equipped 4x4, extra water, cash in XOF, and a trusted local driver-guide.
stopoverNote: Niamey (NIM) is the only practical international gateway. The smoothest loop is Niamey first, then Agadez by bush taxi or limited domestic flight, a 4x4 circuit into the Aïr, and a separate road or guided safari leg south to W National Park.

cities.name: Niamey
cities.notes: National Museum, Grand Marché, riverfront hotels, and the easiest place to sort SIMs, cash, domestic transport, and current security updates

cities.name: Agadez
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed mud-brick old town with the Grand Mosque, Sultan's Palace, caravan history, and classic auberges before heading into the Sahara

cities.name: W National Park
cities.notes: The Niger section of transboundary W Park is best for dry-season wildlife drives, simple camp stays, and guided access from the Say-Tapoa side

cities.name: Aïr Mountains
cities.notes: Remote oasis-and-granite massif reached from Agadez by 4x4, with Timia and Iférouane the classic bases for desert scenery and Tuareg hospitality

itinerary.sim: Airtel Niger is the easiest prepaid tourist pick in Niamey; budget roughly ₹420–₹980 for a starter data bundle, but expect far weaker coverage once you leave the capital and only patchy service around Agadez.

days.theme: Land in the capital, get your bearings, and use Niamey for the practical prep every Niger trip needs right now.

activities.name: Airport taxi from Diori Hamani International Airport to the Plateau or riverfront hotels
National Museum of Niger entry
Grand Marché and Petit Marché browse
City taxi hops between the museum, market, and riverfront
Sunset drink or tea on the Niger River terrace at Grand Hôtel du Niger

hotels.name: Radisson Blu Hotel & Conference Center, Niamey

days.theme: Keep Niamey focused on logistics: cash, SIM, and one last city day before you commit to the desert leg north.

activities.name: Bush taxi seat booking toward Agadez or domestic-flight recheck when a Niamey–Agadez service is operating
Grande Mosquée de Niamey exterior stop and central taxi ride
Craft shopping at Centre Artisanal Wadata
Water, scarf, and cash stock-up for Agadez or W National Park

days.theme: Reach the old caravan city, settle into a Saharan auberge, and explore Agadez's mud-brick core on foot.

activities.name: Niamey–Agadez bush taxi seat or shared transfer
Grand Mosque of Agadez roof / minaret access
activities.tip: The usual quoted ceiling is about 5,000 XOF and access often depends on finding the guardian and coordinating permission.

activities.name: Grand Mosque quarter and old-town walking circuit
Tea stop and market browse around Agadez's historic center

days.theme: Use a second Agadez day for palace access, desert prep, and the seasonal Cure Salée angle if your timing is unusual.

activities.name: Sultan's Palace of Agadez guided visit or guardian fee
activities.tip: There is usually no fixed posted ticket, but a small guide or guardian payment is normally expected.

activities.name: 4x4 quote, fuel, and water loading for the Aïr circuit
Cure Salée festival access at In-Gall (seasonal, usually September)
activities.tip: Festival entry itself is generally free, but you still need transport, camping, and guide logistics from Agadez.

activities.name: 4x4 and guide share from Agadez to In-Gall during Cure Salée

days.theme: Leave the pavement behind with a proper Tuareg-led 4x4 day into the massif, aiming for oasis country rather than rushing the desert.

activities.name: Shared 4x4 with driver-guide from Agadez toward Timia or Iférouane
Campement de Timia room or simple desert-camp stay
Timia oasis walk and village tea stop
Water, picnic, and fuel contribution for the desert leg

days.theme: Keep the Aïr slow and scenic with more oasis time, desert viewpoints, and the kind of buffer that remote Sahara travel always needs.

activities.name: Half-day 4x4 outing to surrounding wadis, granite viewpoints, or palm gardens
Camel ride or local-guide walk near Timia / Iférouane
Second desert-camp night or camp-fire dinner add-on
Return 4x4 share back toward Agadez

days.theme: Treat W as a dry-season safari block: enter early, use a guide, and keep expectations realistic because access and security can change.

activities.name: Road transfer from Niamey or Say to the Tapoa entrance area
W National Park foreign entry permit
4x4 safari vehicle and guide share inside the park
Campement de la Tapoa room or park-side camp stay

meals: Campement de la Tapoa table d'hôte

connections.method: Bush taxi or limited domestic flight
Return via Agadez and Niamey, then road transfer south

extras: Safety advisory: several governments advise against travel to Niger; recheck live advisories before departure and again before every overland leg.
Cure Salée usually runs around September near In-Gall, so it sits outside the cooler Nov–Feb sweet spot even though festival access itself is normally free.
Carry enough XOF cash before leaving Niamey because cards, ATMs, and fuel reliability thin out fast on both the Agadez and W routes.
